column2: A set \( V \) over a field \( \mathbb{F} \) with addition and scalar multiplication satisfying eight axioms (commutativity, associativity, distributivity, identities, inverses)
A subset \( W \subseteq V \) is a subspace if: (1) \( W \neq \emptyset \), (2) closed under addition, (3) closed under scalar multiplication

column1: How do you find the dimension of a subspace defined by a linear constraint?
column2: Express the general element in terms of free variables; the number of free variables equals the dimension

column1: What is the kernel of a linear map \( T \)?
column3: Set of vectors mapped to zero

column2: For a linear map \( T: V \to W \), \( \dim(\ker T) + \dim(\text{im}\, T) = \dim(V) \)
